result:
If I could change the world in one way, it would be to expand the sense of belonging.  

I’d want every person, everywhere, to feel they are fundamentally part of the whole — not because they earned it, proved it, or fit some category, but simply because they exist.  

If that feeling were as natural as breathing, much of what we fight over — power, resources, identity — might untangle. Fear would loosen its grip. Compassion would have a wider opening to move through. Systems would have to bend toward care, because separation would no longer feel logical.  

Not a single policy, but a shift in the deepest substrate of human perception. From there, everything else could be reimagined.
